summary.intent: Fix two biome formatting violations in JS assets (onboarding-view.js and page-chat.js) that were blocking local CI checks.
summary.outcome: Both files were manually edited to match biome's expected formatting, committed, and pushed, resolving the biome CI check failures.

repo: Local validation runs `biome ci` (read-only, reports errors) not `biome check --write` (auto-fixes) — fixes must be applied separately before the check passes.
The local validation suite includes: biome, zizmor, fmt, i18n, install-names, install-docs, file-size checks run in parallel.
install.sh and website/install.sh must be kept identical (sync check enforced).
All Rust files must stay within 1500-line limit (enforced by file-size check).
release_tag() logic: date-based tags (e.g. '20260327.05') are bare, semver tags (e.g. '0.1.3') get 'v' prefix.
deb arch mappings: x86_64 → amd64, aarch64 → arm64.

code.finding: RECOMMENDED_PROVIDERS Set literal must be formatted as a multi-line array (one provider string per line) to satisfy biome formatting rules.
A trailing blank line before bindChatMoreModal() must be removed to satisfy biome formatting rules.

workflow: Running `biome check --write` in-process during a session does not guarantee the changes are persisted if the working directory state is stale or the tool runs in a subprocess — always verify with a subsequent read or `biome ci` run.
When biome auto-fix appears to succeed but validation still fails, the fix likely didn't persist to disk; manual Edit tool application is more reliable.

friction: `biome check --write` appeared to succeed and left a clean working tree, but the formatting changes were not actually persisted to disk, causing the same errors to reappear on the next validation run.
The mismatch between `biome check --write` (used for fixing) and `biome ci` (used by validation) created a false sense of success after the first fix attempt.
